content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation, calls requestWhenInUseAuthorization() and startUpdatingLocation(), and stores route: [CLLocation] (App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-15), and the description.txt explicitly advertises "maps every run with GPS" — a direct contradiction with the fetched privacy policy text.
Pierre: Apple reviewers routinely cross-check the privacy policy against actual app behavior and metadata, and a stated "no location collection" claim next to CLLocationManager usage and GPS-mapping marketing copy is exactly the kind of contradiction that triggers 5.1.1 rejection. You need to update the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection (purpose, retention, whether it leaves the device) or, if truly on-device only, clarify that distinction explicitly rather than denying collection outright. Also verify the App Privacy nutrition label (App Store Connect) and PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy declare Location as a collected/linked type consistent with this usage.
